rulings.Case Title: ERIC LINKER VS. PRIVANTHA RUWIN PERERA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Cross-Defendants Vanguard Properties, Inc., Deborah Nguyen, And Christian Nguyen To Strike Portions Of Privantha Ruwin Pereras Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 8, 2019 line . CROSS DEFENDANT DEBORAH NGUYEN, CHRISTIAN NGUYEN, VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC. Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Cross-Defendants Vanguard Properties, Inc., Deborah Nguyen, And Christian Nguyen To Strike Portions Of Privantha Ruwin Pereras Cross-Complaint is GRANTED with leave to amend for Cross-Complainant to allege any basis for recovery of attorney's fees and litigation costs. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: NORVIN VASQUEZ ET AL VS. ROGER TONNA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION And Notice Of Motion
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 8, 2019 line . DEFENDANT ROGER TONNA, MARY TONNA MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION And Notice Of Motion is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is GRANTED as to issues 2, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 10, 11, 12 - no opposition filed (See Opposition 5:276:4; 6:18-21). Motion is GRANTED as to issue 1. Motion for Summary Adjudication is framed by the complaint. The issue to be determined is whether attorney's fees are available under a cause of action for Implied Breach of Warranty of Habitability. There is no dispute that the lease agreement did not provide for recovery of attorney's fees. There is no other basis for recovery of attorney's fees on a contract-based cause of action. To the extent Plaintiff claims in opposition that attorney's fees are recoverable under for violation of CC 1942.4 (Opposition 6:18), no cause of action for Breach of Warranty of Habitability -Tort/Statute is asserted in the Complaint and Issue 1 does not seek adjudication of availably of attorney's fees under CC 1942.4. Motion is DENIED as to issues 3 and 9. Moving party failed to shift its burden. While the moving party provides a detailed analysis discussing why minor plaintiff is not a tenant, this is not the issue that is being addressed here. Issue 3 addresses availability of attorney's fees under 37.10B. Such fees are available to "prevailing plaintiff" and the civil action may be brought by "any person." Defendant fails to explain why Plaintiff here is not "any person" within the meaning of SFRO or provide any law or fact establishing that "any person" and "tenant" have the same meaning as used in SFRO. Issue 9 addresses availability of treble damages. Treble damages are available to "aggrieved party," there is no analysis of law or fact that "aggrieved party" and "tenant" have the same meaning as used in SFRO. Moving party to prepare order. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: G.J. LEE ET AL VS. LINGXIA XU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant To Provide Further Responses To Form Interrogatories Requests For Production Of Documents And Tangible Things Production Of Documents And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 8, 2019 line . PLAINTIFF G.J. LEE, F.S. LEE Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant To Provide Further Responses To Form Interrogatories Requests For Production Of Documents And Tangible Things Production Of Documents And Request For Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Defendant shall provide responses to Form Interrogatory 2.3 (from 2015 to present); Request for Production 15-18. Motion is DENIED as to Requests for Production 27-30. Sanctions granted in the amount of $2,000. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: FILLMORE MANOR, LLC VS. TALIA KHAYET ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 8, 2019 line . DEFENDANT EMILY HADDAD, CAITLIN BARKER, JULIA LEMKE, RICHARD WOOD, BRYAN QUINTERO, RACHEL AMALIA, TALIA KHAYAT, CHAD BANK DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is continued to February 8, 2020 with briefings due on the same timeline as outlined in this Court's September 10, 2019. Parties may appear ex-parte to seek a continuance of the hearing if the Rent Board does not render a decision at least 5 business days before the first supplemental brief is due. =(501/CFH)
